# P3.5 — Auto-generated table of contents

## Priority: P3

## Problem

Long policy documents (HMS handbook, privacy policy) have many sections but no
nav to jump between them. An auto-generated table of contents from heading
blocks would make long documents much more navigable.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `WikiPageView.tsx` renders a floating or sticky TOC panel for pages with
  3 or more heading blocks
- [ ] The TOC lists all `HeadingBlock` items with anchor links
- [ ] Clicking a TOC entry scrolls the page to that heading
- [ ] The currently visible heading is highlighted in the TOC (scroll spy)
- [ ] The TOC is hidden on mobile (viewport < 768 px)
- [ ] The TOC is hidden when there are fewer than 3 headings
- [ ] No new dependencies required
- [ ] `npm run build` exits 0, zero TS errors

## Files to modify

| File | Change |
|---|---|
| `src/pages/documents/WikiPageView.tsx` | Extract headings, render TOC, scroll spy |
| `src/pages/documents/WikiBlockRenderer.tsx` | Add `id` attribute to rendered headings |

## Files to read first

- `src/pages/documents/WikiPageView.tsx` — full file; understand layout structure
- `src/pages/documents/WikiBlockRenderer.tsx` — heading block render case
- `src/types/documents.ts` — `HeadingBlock`

## Implementation

### Step 1 — Add id to rendered headings

In `WikiBlockRenderer.tsx`, find the heading case. Add a deterministic `id`:

```tsx
case 'heading': {
  const id = `heading-${block.text.toLowerCase().replace(/[^a-z0-9]+/g, '-').replace(/(^-|-$)/g, '')}`
  const Tag = `h${block.level}` as 'h1' | 'h2' | 'h3'
  return <Tag id={id} key={idx} className={/* existing classes */}>{block.text}</Tag>
}
```

### Step 2 — Extract heading list in WikiPageView

```tsx
const headings = useMemo(
  () =>
    page.blocks
      .filter((b): b is HeadingBlock => b.kind === 'heading')
      .map((b) => ({
        text: b.text,
        level: b.level,
        id: `heading-${b.text.toLowerCase().replace(/[^a-z0-9]+/g, '-').replace(/(^-|-$)/g, '')}`,
      })),
  [page.blocks]
)
```

### Step 3 — Scroll spy with IntersectionObserver

```tsx
const [activeId, setActiveId] = useState<string | null>(null)

useEffect(() => {
  if (headings.length < 3) return
  const observer = new IntersectionObserver(
    (entries) => {
      const visible = entries.filter((e) => e.isIntersecting)
      if (visible.length > 0) setActiveId(visible[0]!.target.id)
    },
    { rootMargin: '-10% 0px -80% 0px', threshold: 0 }
  )
  headings.forEach(({ id }) => {
    const el = document.getElementById(id)
    if (el) observer.observe(el)
  })
  return () => observer.disconnect()
}, [headings])
```

### Step 4 — TOC panel JSX

Render the TOC in a `<aside>` positioned sticky on the right side, visible only
on `md:` and above:

```tsx
{headings.length >= 3 && (
  <aside className="hidden md:block sticky top-24 h-fit w-52 shrink-0">
    <p className="mb-2 text-xs font-semibold uppercase tracking-wide text-neutral-500">
      Innhold
    </p>
    <nav>
      <ul className="space-y-1">
        {headings.map((h) => (
          <li
            key={h.id}
            style={{ paddingLeft: `${(h.level - 1) * 12}px` }}
          >
            <a
              href={`#${h.id}`}
              className={`block truncate text-xs leading-5 ${
                activeId === h.id
                  ? 'font-semibold text-[#1a3d32]'
                  : 'text-neutral-500 hover:text-neutral-800'
              }`}
              onClick={(e) => {
                e.preventDefault()
                document.getElementById(h.id)?.scrollIntoView({ behavior: 'smooth' })
              }}
            >
              {h.text}
            </a>
          </li>
        ))}
      </ul>
    </nav>
  </aside>
)}
```

Adjust the main content area to use a two-column layout when the TOC is shown:
```tsx
<div className={headings.length >= 3 ? 'flex gap-8' : undefined}>
  <article className="min-w-0 flex-1">{… existing content …}</article>
  {… TOC aside …}
</div>
```

## Validation checklist

- [ ] `npm run build` exits 0, zero TS errors
- [ ] No new packages added
- [ ] Pages with ≥3 headings show the TOC panel on desktop
- [ ] Pages with <3 headings do not show the TOC
- [ ] Clicking a TOC entry scrolls to the correct heading
- [ ] The active heading is highlighted as the user scrolls
- [ ] TOC is hidden on mobile (viewport < 768 px)
- [ ] Heading `id` attributes are deterministic and URL-safe
- [ ] File reads completed before editing
